summary: Kalpen Parekh, MD & CEO of DSP Mutual Fund, said something most fund managers would never dare say on camera: "If someone gives you a fund recommendation without understanding your time horizon, your risk appetite, and your current portfolio, that is fraught with risk."

seo.title: Buy Good, Buy Cheap, Stay Long, Kalpen Parekh, DSP Mutual Fund
seo.description: Kalpen Parekh, MD & CEO of DSP Mutual Fund, said something most fund managers would never dare say on camera: "If someone gives you a fund

takeaways.text: Kalpen Parekh, MD and CEO of DSP Mutual Fund, says a fund recommendation given without understanding your time horizon, your risk appetite and your current portfolio is fraught with risk.
"Which fund should I buy?" is almost always the wrong question, because it skips the personal context that decides whether a fund actually fits you.
Indian investors keep losing money even when they know the right theory, so the real gap is usually behaviour, not knowledge.
Good investing comes down to three rules: quality, valuation and time horizon. Buy good and hold, and if you buy bad, do not hold even for a day.

With India and the US among the most expensive markets, equities in a correction and gold at all-time highs, Kalpen leans on multi-asset investing precisely because you cannot know in advance what will win.
